channel.title: Json on Margrop Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Json on Margrop Blog

item.title: Feign usage examples: @Body annotation, http request body
item.description: There is a situation where the interface uses the http request body to pass parameters, so I studied feign and found that the @Body annotation can generally be implemented. Although it is said that the request body is used to pass parameters, in fact, the request body still contains data in json format. Of course, if you don’t want to be limited to the json format, you can set it to a universal format. See the end of the article for details.

item.title: How to Map JSON Objects Using Generic Hibernate Types
item.description:
Introduction

In this article, we’ll see how to map JSON columns to JPA entity attributes using the Hibernate Types open-source project.

While you can create your own custom Hibernate type to map JSON column types on Oracle, SQL Server, PostgreSQL, or MySQL, you don’t need to implement your own Hibernate Type since the Hibernate Types project already provides this functionality.
